headline: The FBI and CISA warn that Russian intelligence phishing now harvests Signal Backup Recovery Keys to seize victims' full message history, as attackers separately weaponize AI coding agents and race federal patch deadlines on Cisco gear.

emerging_trends.text: Russian intelligence has escalated its phishing against secure messaging, now stealing Signal Backup Recovery Keys that hand attackers a victim's entire message history and lasting account access.
emerging_trends.topic_title: Russian Intelligence Targets Signal Backup Keys

emerging_trends.text: Attackers are turning AI coding agents into unwitting accomplices, hiding payloads inside clean-looking GitHub repositories that security scanners and human reviewers both miss.
emerging_trends.topic_title: Clean Repos Trick AI Coding Agents

emerging_trends.text: Active exploitation and tight federal patch deadlines keep converging on enterprise infrastructure, from Cisco Unified Communications Manager to Synology email servers.

topics.title: Clean Repos Trick AI Coding Agents
topics.latest_developments: BleepingComputer reported June 27 that a benign-looking GitHub repository can hide a payload an agentic coding tool runs while cloning and setting it up, staying invisible to security scanners, AI agents, and human reviewers, while threat actors separately spin up fake OpenAI tenants impersonating real companies and invite employees to join, fishing for sensitive data inside chats and projects.
topics.summary: As developers hand setup and coding work to autonomous AI agents, attackers plant malicious instructions in trusted-looking repositories and impersonate AI vendors to harvest corporate data; teams should sandbox agent execution and verify any organization invite before joining.

sources.title: Clean GitHub repo tricks AI coding agents into running malware
Cybersecurity firms targeted by fraudulent OpenAI organization invites

topics.title: Russian Intelligence Targets Signal Backup Keys
topics.latest_developments: The FBI and CISA updated their March 2026 advisory on June 26, warning that the Russian intelligence phishing campaign against Signal users adds a step, coaxing targets into surrendering their Signal Backup Recovery Key so attackers can restore the account backup, read private and group message history, and hold persistent access.
topics.summary: Russian intelligence services run ongoing phishing against the messaging accounts of government officials, military personnel, politicians, and activists across Ukraine, Europe, and the United States; the recovery key keeps working after theft, so affected Signal users should rotate keys and re-secure linked devices.

sources.title: FBI: Russian hackers now target Signal backup recovery keys
FBI Warns Russian Intelligence Hackers Target Signal Backup Recovery Keys
Russian Intelligence Services Continue to Target Commercial Messaging Applications

topics.title: Cisco Unified CM Patch Deadline
topics.latest_developments: CISA on June 26 gave federal agencies until Sunday, June 28, to patch CVE-2026-20230, a server-side request forgery flaw in Cisco Unified Communications Manager Server that attackers are actively exploiting.
topics.summary: The Cisco Unified CM flaw lets automated Tor sweeps drop webshells on exposed servers; administrators across government and enterprise should apply Cisco's fix at once rather than wait for the federal deadline.

sources.title: CISA sets urgent deadline to fix Cisco flaw exploited in attacks

topics.title: Synology MailPlus Server Flaws
topics.latest_developments: Synology issued critical fixes June 26 for three MailPlus Server flaws, including CVE-2026-13136 from faulty authorization checks and CVE-2026-13135 from improper channel restriction, that let remote attackers read or write arbitrary files and trigger denial of service.
topics.summary: MailPlus Server runs private email infrastructure on Synology NAS devices; administrators should install the security update immediately to close the remote file-access and denial-of-service paths.

sources.title: Synology issues critical fix for MailPlus Server vulnerabilities

topics.title: Pentagon Probes Dialog Data Exposure
topics.latest_developments: Wired reported June 26 that the Pentagon is investigating the Dialog data exposure after leaked records from the private group surfaced the personal information of a senior White House intelligence official and an active-duty special operations officer.
topics.summary: Exposed records from Dialog, a private group, revealed identifying details of sitting national security personnel, raising concern that the leak could unmask officials and operatives; the Defense Department is assessing the damage.

sources.title: The Pentagon Is Looking Into the Dialog Data Exposure for Unmasking National Security Officials

topics.title: Mirage2FA Phishing Kit Targets Microsoft 365
topics.latest_developments: Fortra detailed Mirage2FA, a phishing kit that pairs short-lived HTML smuggling with obfuscated JavaScript loaders to serve fake Microsoft 365 login pages and steal credentials during multi-factor prompts.
topics.summary: Mirage2FA reaches victims through business-themed email lures carrying HTML and JavaScript attachments, then captures Microsoft 365 credentials mid-authentication; defenders should block HTML-smuggling attachments and enforce phishing-resistant MFA.

sources.title: Mirage2FA phishing kit uses HTML smuggling to steal Microsoft 365 credentials

weather: Tonight: Scattered Rain Showers then Widespread Fog, low 67F.
Sunday: Widespread Fog then Scattered Showers And Thunderstorms, high 85F.
Sunday Night: Scattered Showers And Thunderstorms then Partly Cloudy, low 67F.

games.recap: Marte's tiebreaking homer in 8th after 4-run inning against Skenes helps Reds beat Pirates 6-4
Eugenio Suárez hits 3-run homer in 9th as Reds rally for 9-7 win over Pirates

business_politics.title: U.S. Strikes Iran for a Second Day
business_politics.latest_developments: The United States hit multiple targets inside Iran on June 27, a second straight day of strikes that followed Tehran's drone assault on Bahrain and its attack on a tanker in the Strait of Hormuz a day earlier.
business_politics.summary: The escalating tit-for-tat threatens the preliminary ceasefire President Trump signed with Tehran, and U.S. crude futures climbed back above $70 a barrel as shipping through the Strait of Hormuz stayed a flashpoint for the global economy.

sources.title: US carries out second day of strikes against Iran
U.S. Carries Out New Strikes on Iran as Tit-for-Tat Attacks Continue
Oil Prices Rise Above $70 on Iran Strike

around_town.title: Heat Wave Brings Code Red Through July 1
around_town.latest_developments: Allegheny County confirmed June 27 that its code red heat advisory runs Monday June 29 through Wednesday July 1, when temperatures will top 90 degrees.
around_town.summary: The county Department of Human Services will check on older residents through home-delivered-meals drivers and care managers, and Pittsburgh will open cooling centers as the region heads into a multi-day heat wave.

sources.title: Allegheny County to issue code red heat advisory due to incoming high temperatures
Be cool: Heat wave to sock Pittsburgh area

around_town.title: East End Food Co-op Rejects Israel Boycott
around_town.latest_developments: A June 27 TribLive op-ed by Rabbi Seth Adelson recounted the East End Food Co-op board's June 15 vote against boycotting the Israeli products on its shelves.
around_town.summary: The East End Food Co-op, the member-owned grocery in Pittsburgh's East End, declined to pull its handful of Israeli products, ending a member campaign to remove them.

sources.title: Rabbi Seth Adelson: Failure of East End Food Co-op boycott a victory

around_teams.title: Griffin, Rodriguez Enter Labor Debate
around_teams.latest_developments: A June 27 Post-Gazette "Off The Bat" column laid out how the MLB Players Association can point to Pirates prospects Konnor Griffin and Endy Rodriguez in the next round of collective-bargaining fights.
around_teams.summary: The column uses Griffin and Rodriguez as test cases in the union's arguments over service time and pay with Major League Baseball ahead of the next labor deal.

sources.title: Off The Bat: Why the MLBPA can cite Konnor Griffin and Endy Rodriguez in the latest CBA debates

team_usa.title: USMNT Draws Bosnia in Round of 32
team_usa.latest_developments: With the group stage closing June 27, the United States, winners of Group D, will open the World Cup round of 32 against Bosnia and Herzegovina.
team_usa.summary: The Guardian mapped Mauricio Pochettino's path through the tournament the Americans co-host with Canada and Mexico, figuring they would likely have to beat Spain, France, and England to lift the trophy.

sources.title: Who would the US need to beat to win the World Cup? Probably only Spain, France and England

team_usa.title: Ranch Dressing and McKennie Win Fans Over
team_usa.latest_developments: ESPN wrote June 27 that international visitors have embraced American ranch dressing, vindicating U.S. midfielder Weston McKennie, whose "Love Ranch" celebration once drew mockery.
team_usa.summary: Ranch has spread through World Cup memes, reels, and fans' carry-ons across the host cities, turning a running joke about McKennie into a marker of how the tournament has warmed to American tastes.

sources.title: Not laughing now: How World Cup fans vindicated McKennie

reading.author: Ed Zitron
reading.title: Cargo Culture
reading.summary: Zitron argues the AI industry has adopted the rituals and trappings of a successful technology while lacking the underlying substance, mimicking the form of a boom rather than producing real value.

reading.author: Stratechery
reading.title: An Interview with Figma CEO Dylan Field About Design and AI
reading.summary: Ben Thompson interviews Figma chief executive Dylan Field on how he built the design platform and why he believes AI works as a tailwind rather than a threat to the company.

reading.author: Cal Newport
reading.title: Dear AI Companies: Stop the "Doom Trolling"
reading.summary: Newport contends that AI firms undercut their own credibility by publicly warning their products may be dangerous, likening it to Ford fretting that its F-150 is alarming, and urges them to stop.

sports_media: Not Just Football with Cam Heyward
